# Apartment AI summary ‚Äî design

**Issue:** [#54 ‚Äî Add an auto-generated short description/summary in English (translate if necessary) to the apartment page](https://github.com/brlauuu/flatpare/issues/54)
**Date:** 2026-04-25

## Problem

Listing PDFs typically include a paragraph or two of prose ‚Äî neighborhood character, layout, views, condition ‚Äî that gets discarded today by the structured extractor. The user wants a 1‚Äì2 sentence English summary surfaced on the apartment detail page, translated from German when needed.

## Scope

- AI extraction adds a `summary` field (English, 1‚Äì2 sentences, ~25‚Äì40 words).
- DB column `summary text` (nullable).
- Detail page renders the summary in a card between the header and the metric cards. Hidden when null.
- Editable in the existing edit form via a `<Textarea>`.
- List card and compare view are unchanged.

## Storage

`text` column on the apartments table, nullable:

```ts
// src/lib/db/schema.ts
summary: text("summary"),
```

Migration via `npm run db:generate` + `npm run db:push`. Non-destructive (adds a nullable column).

## API & form types

- `apartmentExtractionSchema` (in `src/lib/parse-pdf.ts`) gains `summary: string | null`.
- `ApartmentForm` (in `src/components/apartment-form-fields.tsx`) gains `summary: string` (empty when not set).
- `emptyApartmentForm` adds `summary: ""`.
- `ApartmentLike` adds `summary: string | null`.
- `formFromExtracted`, `formFromApartment`, `formToPayload` round-trip the field.
- `ApartmentDetail` (in `src/app/apartments/[id]/page.tsx`) adds `summary: string | null`.
- `ApartmentSummary` (list page) and `ApartmentWithRatings` (compare): NOT updated. The summary is not displayed in those views.

API routes:

- `POST /api/apartments`: accept `summary` from body, insert.
- `PATCH /api/apartments/[id]`: accept `summary`, update.
- `GET /api/apartments` (list): explicitly select `summary` (the existing query enumerates fields). The list cards don't render it, but adding it to the response keeps the type contract clean.
- `GET /api/apartments/[id]` (detail): uses `select()` (full row) ‚Äî auto-includes once the column exists.

## AI extraction

Schema addition in `apartmentExtractionSchema`:

```ts
summary: z
  .string()
  .nullable()
  .describe(
    "A 1-2 sentence English summary of the apartment (~25-40 words). " +
    "Translate from German if needed. Highlight the most distinctive features: " +
    "neighborhood, layout style, view, condition, or notable amenities. " +
    "null only if the listing has no descriptive content beyond the bare facts."
  ),
```

Prompt addition (appended to the existing prompt):

> For `summary`: write a 1‚Äì2 sentence English summary (~25‚Äì40 words) of what the apartment is like. Translate from German if needed. Pick out distinctive features ‚Äî neighborhood character, layout, views, condition, or notable amenities ‚Äî rather than just restating the metric fields. Return null only if the listing has no descriptive content.

No server-side post-processing. If the model overshoots the word budget, we accept the result ‚Äî trimming would be premature optimization.

## UI

### Detail page summary card

Place between the existing header block (ShortCode + name + address + action buttons) and the existing metric/details cards. Renders only when `apartment.summary` is non-null:

```tsx
{apartment.summary && (
  <Card>
    <CardContent className="p-4">
      <p className="text-sm leading-relaxed">{apartment.summary}</p>
    </CardContent>
  </Card>
)}
```

The card matches the visual pattern of the other Card components on the page. The summary sets prose context before the numeric metrics, mirroring how a typical listing reads top-to-bottom.

### Edit form textarea

Add a `<Textarea>` to `ApartmentFormFields`. Place it above the `name` field (the summary is the most narrative input and naturally goes first):

```tsx
<div className="space-y-1.5">
  <Label htmlFor={`${idPrefix}-summary`}>Summary</Label>
  <Textarea
    id={`${idPrefix}-summary`}
    value={form.summary}
    onChange={(e) => onChange("summary", e.target.value)}
    rows={3}
    placeholder="Short English description (auto-generated by AI)"
  />
</div>
```

3 rows fits ~40 words without scrolling. shadcn `<Textarea>` is already used in the rating form.

### Other views

- List card (`/apartments`): unchanged ‚Äî no summary shown.
- Compare view (`/compare`): unchanged.

## Testing

### Unit tests in `src/lib/__tests__/parse-pdf.test.ts` (extend, 5 new tests)

In the existing `describe("apartmentExtractionSchema", ...)` block:

1. Schema accepts a sample summary string.
2. Schema accepts `summary: null`.
3. Schema rejects `summary: 123` (non-string).

New `describe("extractApartmentData ‚Äî summary passthrough", ...)` block:

4. AI returns a summary ‚Üí result has the same value.
5. AI returns `summary: null` ‚Üí result has `null`.

### Integration tests in `src/app/apartments/[id]/__tests__/edit-flow.test.tsx` (extend, 2 new tests)

Update `APARTMENT_V1` to include `summary: "Quiet 2.5-room flat in a leafy district near transit."`. `APARTMENT_V2` gets a rewritten summary string.

6. **Read-only display when set** ‚Äî assert the V1 summary text is in the document.
7. **Edit round-trips the summary** ‚Äî open edit form, change the textarea value, save; assert PATCH body contains the new summary string.

The 2nd new test gets a `10000ms` timeout ‚Äî same pattern as the existing "save flips name + rent + washing" test, since multiple userEvent operations.

### No upload-page test

The retry test in `src/app/apartments/new/__tests__/retry.test.tsx` already mocks the parse-pdf response. Adding `summary` to its mock just to assert it lands in the form is low value; `formFromExtracted` is covered indirectly by the parse-pdf tests.

### Existing tests

Unchanged. The list/compare/pager/search/retry tests don't reference `summary`.

## Migration & rollout

- `npm run db:generate` creates a migration adding the `summary` column.
- `npm run db:push` applies it locally.
- Existing apartments have `summary: null`. Re-uploading regenerates, or the user types one manually via the edit form.

## Out of scope

- "Regenerate" button on the detail page (rejected by Q2: editable-only flow).
- Showing the summary on list cards or compare view.
- Multi-language output ‚Äî English only.
- Source-language detection metadata.
- Server-side length validation. We trust the AI.

## Security notes

- Summary is rendered as plain text inside `<p>` (React escapes by default ‚Äî no XSS risk).
- No new endpoints, trust boundaries, or user-controlled inputs beyond what already exists.
